    99→        return;
   100→      }
   101→      submitLayoutInstruction(instruction);
   102→    });
   103→  }
   104→
   105→  if (proceedBtn) {
   106→    proceedBtn.addEventListener('click', completeLayoutReview);
   107→  }
   108→
   109→  if (historyToggle) {
   110→    historyToggle.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
   111→      e.target.textContent = e.target.textContent === '▼' ? '▶' : '▼';
   112→      const historyList = document.getElementById('instruction-history');
   113→      if (historyList) {
   114→        historyList.classList.toggle('collapsed');
   115→      }
   116→    });
   117→  }
   118→
   119→  // Allow Enter key to submit in textarea (Shift+Enter for new line)
   120→  if (instructionInput) {
   121→    instructionInput.addEventListener('keypress', (e) => {
   122→      if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {
   123→        e.preventDefault();
   124→        applyBtn?.click();
   125→      }
   126→    });
   127→  }
   128→}
   129→
   130→/**
   131→ * Submit layout instruction to backend for processing.
   132→ *
   133→ * Uses POST /api/cv/layout-refine (staged generation contract) when a
   134→ * session-stored preview is available.  Falls back to the legacy
   135→ * POST /api/layout-instruction endpoint (which requires the HTML in the
   136→ * request body) when no session preview exists.
   137→ */
   138→async function submitLayoutInstruction(instructionText) {
   139→  const currentHtml = window.tabData?.cv?.['*.html'] || '';
   140→  const priorInstructions = window.layoutInstructions || [];
   141→
   142→  try {
   143→    showProcessing(true);
   144→
   145→    // Prefer the session-backed endpoint; it manages HTML server-side.
   146→    let response;
   147→    const genState = stateManager?.getGenerationState?.() || {};
   148→    const useSessionEndpoint = genState.previewAvailable || genState.phase === 'layout_review';
   149→
   150→    if (useSessionEndpoint) {
   151→      response = await apiCall('POST', '/api/cv/layout-refine', {
   152→        instruction: instructionText,
   153→      });
   154→    } else {
   155→      response = await apiCall('POST', '/api/layout-instruction', {
   156→        instruction: instructionText,
   157→        current_html: currentHtml,
   158→        prior_instructions: priorInstructions,
   159→      });
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    if (!response.ok) {
   163→      if (response.error === 'clarify') {
   164→        showClarificationDialog(response.question, instructionText);
   165→      } else {
   166→        let errorHtml = `⚠️ Error: ${htmlEscape(response.error)} — ${htmlEscape(response.details || '')}`;
   167→        if (response.raw_response !== undefined) {
   168→          errorHtml += `<br><details style="margin-top:6px"><summary style="cursor:pointer;font-size:0.85em;color:#64748b">Raw LLM response</summary><pre style="font-size:0.75em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-all;max-height:200px;overflow-y:auto;background:#f8fafc;border:1px solid #e2e8f0;border-radius:4px;padding:8px;margin-top:4px">${htmlEscape(response.raw_response || '(empty)')}</pre></details>`;
   169→        }
   170→        appendMessageHtml('system', errorHtml);
   171→      }
   172→      return;
   173→    }
   174→
   175→    // Update preview with new HTML
   176→    const newHtml = response.html;
   177→    displayLayoutPreview(newHtml);
   178→
   179→    // Update state
   180→    window.tabData.cv['*.html'] = newHtml;
   181→
   182→    // Add to instruction history
   183→    const instruction = {
   184→      timestamp: new Date().toLocaleTimeString(),
   185→      instruction_text: instructionText,
   186→      change_summary: response.summary,
   187→      confirmation: true
   188→    };
   189→    addToInstructionHistory(instruction);
   190→
   191→    // Show confirmation
   192→    showConfirmationMessage(`✅ ${response.summary}`);
   193→
   194→    // Clear input and show proceed button
   195→    document.getElementById('instruction-input').value = '';
   196→    document.getElementById('proceed-to-finalise-btn').style.display = 'block';
   197→
   198→  } catch (error) {
   199→    appendMessage('system', `❌ Failed to apply layout instruction: ${error.message}`);
   200→  } finally {
   201→    showProcessing(false);
   202→  }
   203→}
   204→
   205→/**
   206→ * Fetch the CV HTML preview via the staged generation contract.
   207→ *
   208→ * First tries POST /api/cv/generate-preview (renders fresh HTML from current
   209→ * session state and stores it).  Falls back to GET /api/layout-html (legacy
   210→ * endpoint that reads the most recent HTML file from disk) when the session
   211→ * does not yet have customization data.
   212→ */
   213→async function _fetchAndDisplayLayoutPreview() {
   214→  // Try staged generation endpoint first
   215→  try {
   216→    const data = await apiCall('POST', '/api/cv/generate-preview', {});
   217→    if (data.ok && data.html) {
   218→      displayLayoutPreview(data.html);
   219→      if (!window.tabData) window.tabData = {};
   220→      if (!window.tabData.cv || typeof window.tabData.cv !== 'object') {
   221→        window.tabData.cv = {};
   222→      }
   223→      window.tabData.cv['*.html'] = data.html;
   224→      return;
   225→    }
   226→  } catch (_e) {
   227→    // fall through to legacy endpoint
   228→  }
   229→
   230→  // Legacy fallback: load HTML from the output directory on disk
   231→  try {
   232→    const data = await apiCall('GET', '/api/layout-html');
   233→    if (data.ok && data.html) {
   234→      displayLayoutPreview(data.html);
   235→      if (!window.tabData) window.tabData = {};
   236→      if (!window.tabData.cv || typeof window.tabData.cv !== 'object') {
   237→        window.tabData.cv = {};
   238→      }
   239→      window.tabData.cv['*.html'] = data.html;
   240→    } else {
   241→      console.warn('Layout preview not available:', data.error || 'no HTML returned');
   242→    }
   243→  } catch (err) {
   244→    console.warn('Could not load layout preview:', err);
   245→  }
   246→}
   247→
   248→/**
   249→ * Display HTML preview in iframe.
   250→ */
   251→function displayLayoutPreview(html) {
   252→  const preview = document.getElementById('layout-preview');
   253→  if (!preview) return;
   254→
   255→  preview.onload = () => fitLayoutPreviewToPane(preview);
   256→
   257→  // Set iframe content safely
   258→  const doc = preview.contentDocument || preview.contentWindow?.document;
   259→  if (doc) {
   260→    doc.open();
   261→    doc.write(html);
   262→    doc.close();
   263→    fitLayoutPreviewToPane(preview);
   264→  }
   265→}
   266→
   267→/**
   268→ * Scale the preview so an entire CV page width fits within the preview pane.
   269→ */
   270→function fitLayoutPreviewToPane(preview) {
   271→  const doc = preview?.contentDocument || preview?.contentWindow?.document;
   272→  const container = preview?.closest('.preview-iframe-container');
   273→  if (!doc || !container) return;
   274→
   275→  const pageContainer = doc.querySelector('.page-container') || doc.body;
   276→  if (!pageContainer) return;
   277→
   278→  const containerWidth = Math.max(container.clientWidth - 24, 1);
   279→  const contentWidth = Math.max(
   280→    Math.ceil(pageContainer.scrollWidth || 0),
   281→    Math.ceil(pageContainer.getBoundingClientRect().width || 0),
   282→    1
   283→  );
   284→  const scale = Math.min(1, containerWidth / contentWidth);
   285→
   286→  doc.documentElement.style.background = '#f8fafc';
   287→  doc.body.style.margin = '0';
   288→  doc.body.style.padding = '0';
   289→  doc.body.style.background = '#f8fafc';
   290→  doc.body.style.overflowX = 'auto';
   291→
   292→  pageContainer.style.zoom = `${scale}`;
   293→  pageContainer.style.transform = '';
   294→  pageContainer.style.transformOrigin = '';
   295→  pageContainer.style.margin = '12px';
   296→  preview.style.minWidth = '';
   297→}
   298→
   299→/**
   300→ * Add instruction to history panel.
   301→ */
   302→function addToInstructionHistory(instruction) {
   303→  // Initialize global instruction list if needed
   304→  if (!window.layoutInstructions) {
   305→    window.layoutInstructions = [];
   306→  }
   307→
   308→  window.layoutInstructions.push(instruction);
   309→  renderInstructionHistory();
   310→}
   311→
   312→/**
   313→ * Render instruction history from current state without mutating it.
   314→ */
   315→function renderInstructionHistory() {
   316→  const historyList = document.getElementById('instruction-history');
   317→  if (!historyList) return;
   318→
   319→  historyList.innerHTML = '';
   320→  (window.layoutInstructions || []).forEach((instruction, index) => {
   321→    const entry = document.createElement('div');
   322→    entry.className = 'instruction-history-entry';
   323→    entry.innerHTML = `
   324→      <div class="instruction-time">${instruction.timestamp || ''}</div>
   325→      <div class="instruction-text">${htmlEscape(instruction.instruction_text || '')}</div>
   326→      <div class="instruction-summary"><em>${htmlEscape(instruction.change_summary || '')}</em></div>
   327→      <button class="btn btn-small" onclick="undoInstruction(${index})">
   328→        Undo
   329→      </button>
   330→    `;
   331→
   332→    historyList.appendChild(entry);
   333→  });
   334→
   335→  // Update count
   336→  document.getElementById('instruction-count').textContent = (window.layoutInstructions || []).length;
   337→}
   338→
   339→/**
   340→ * Restore instruction history from session state.
   341→ */
   342→function restoreInstructionHistory() {
   343→  renderInstructionHistory();
   344→
   345→  // Show proceed button if any instructions applied
   346→  const instructions = window.layoutInstructions || [];
   347→  if (instructions.length > 0) {
   348→    document.getElementById('proceed-to-finalise-btn').style.display = 'block';
   349→  }
   350→}
   351→
   352→/**
   353→ * Show processing spinner.
   354→ */
   355→function showProcessing(show) {
   356→  const indicator = document.getElementById('processing-indicator');
   357→  if (indicator) {
   358→    indicator.style.display = show ? 'block' : 'none';
   359→  }
   360→}
   361→
   362→/**
   363→ * Show confirmation message.
   364→ */
   365→function showConfirmationMessage(message) {
   366→  const element = document.getElementById('confirmation-message');
   367→  if (!element) return;
   368→
   369→  element.textContent = message;
   370→  element.style.display = 'block';
   371→
   372→  // Auto-hide after 3 seconds
   373→  setTimeout(() => {
   374→    element.style.display = 'none';
   375→  }, 3000);
   376→}
   377→
   378→/**
   379→ * Show inline clarification dialog when LLM needs more info.
   380→ */
   381→function showClarificationDialog(question, originalInstruction) {
   382→  const response = prompt(
   383→    `The system needs clarification:\n\n${question}\n\nYour original: "${originalInstruction}"\n\nPlease clarify:`,
   384→    originalInstruction
   385→  );
   386→
   387→  if (response && response !== originalInstruction) {
   388→    submitLayoutInstruction(response);
   389→  }
   390→}
   391→
   392→/**
   393→ * Undo a specific instruction (regenerate from prior step).
   394→ */
   395→function undoInstruction(index) {
   396→  if (!window.layoutInstructions || index < 0 || index >= window.layoutInstructions.length) {
   397→    return;
   398→  }
   399→
   400→  window.layoutInstructions.splice(index, 1);
   401→
   402→  // Regenerate preview from HTML at this point
   403→  // (simplified: in production, would re-apply all prior instructions)
   404→  appendMessage('system', '🔄 Undo not yet implemented — would regenerate from prior state');
   405→
   406→  // Update history display
   407→  const historyList = document.getElementById('instruction-history');
   408→  if (historyList) {
   409→    renderInstructionHistory();
   410→  }
   411→}
   412→
   413→window.addEventListener('resize', () => {
   414→  const preview = document.getElementById('layout-preview');
   415→  if (preview) {
   416→    fitLayoutPreviewToPane(preview);
   417→  }
   418→});
   419→
   420→/**
   421→ * Complete layout review: confirm layout via staged generation contract,
   422→ * trigger final PDF/DOCX generation from the confirmed HTML, then advance
   423→ * the conversation phase via the legacy /api/layout-complete endpoint.
   424→ */
   425→async function completeLayoutReview() {
   426→  try {
   427→    showProcessing(true);
   428→
   429→    // Confirm layout and generate final outputs when staged flow is active (GAP-20).
   430→    const genState = stateManager?.getGenerationState?.() || {};
   431→    if (genState.previewAvailable || genState.phase === 'layout_review') {
   432→      try {
   433→        await apiCall('POST', '/api/cv/confirm-layout', {});
   434→      } catch (_e) {
   435→        // non-fatal: continue to final generation attempt
   436→      }
   437→
   438→      // Produce final PDF/DOCX from the confirmed HTML.
   439→      try {
   440→        const finalRes = await apiCall('POST', '/api/cv/generate-final', {});
   441→        if (finalRes && finalRes.ok && finalRes.outputs) {
   442→          if (!window.tabData) window.tabData = {};
   443→          window.tabData.cv = finalRes.outputs;
   444→          stateManager?.setGenerationState?.({ phase: 'final_complete' });
   445→        }
   446→      } catch (_e) {
   447→        // non-fatal: legacy outputs remain available for download
   448→      }
   449→
   450→      // Refresh ATS badge after final generation (GAP-21).
   451→      if (typeof scheduleAtsRefresh === 'function') {
   452→        scheduleAtsRefresh('post_generation');
   453→      }
   454→    }
   455→
   456→    const response = await apiCall('POST', '/api/layout-complete', {
   457→      layout_instructions: window.layoutInstructions || []
   458→    });
   459→
   460→    if (!response.ok) {
   461→      appendMessage('system', `❌ Error: ${response.error}`);
   462→      return;
   463→    }
   464→
   465→    appendMessage('assistant', '✅ Layout confirmed and final output generated.');
   466→
   467→    // Update phase and switch to download/generation tab
   468→    stateManager.setPhase('refinement');
   469→    switchTab('download');
   470→
   471→  } catch (error) {
   472→    appendMessage('system', `❌ Failed to complete layout review: ${error.message}`);
   473→  } finally {
   474→    showProcessing(false);
   475→  }
   476→}
   477→
```
</details></tool-use>

### 3. ATS Score API Endpoint

**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/.claude/worktrees/gh-issues/scripts/web_app.py` (lines 4846–4919)

**Endpoint**: `POST /api/cv/ats-score`

**Function Signature**:
```python
@app.post("/api/cv/ats-score")
def compute_cv_ats_score():
    """Return ATS match score for current session state (GAP-21).
    
    Computes the score from job_analysis and customizations stored in
    the session.  Optionally re-persists the score in generation_state.
    
    Request body:
        { "session_id": str, "basis": str | None }
    
    Response:
        { "ok": True, "ats_score": { ...Phase-0 contract schema... } }
    """
```

**What It Returns**:

The `ats_score` object follows this Phase-0 contract schema (from `/scripts/utils/scoring.py`, lines 348–356):

```python
{
    "overall": float,                    # 0-100 overall match score
    "hard_requirement_score": float,     # score for required skills
    "soft_requirement_score": float,     # score for nice-to-have skills
    "keyword_status": [...],             # list of keyword match details
    "section_scores": {...},             # scores by section (skills, experience, education, summary)
    "computed_at": str,                  # ISO-8601 timestamp
    "basis": str                         # "analysis", "review_checkpoint", or "post_generation"
}
```

The function:
- Fetches `job_analysis` and `customizations` from session state
- Enriches customizations with skill decisions, rewritten bullets, and summary overrides
- Calls `compute_ats_score()` utility function
- Persists score into `generation_state` for client polling
- Returns the computed score object

### 4. ATS Score Polling/Display in JavaScript

**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/.claude/worktrees/gh-issues/web/app.js` (lines 10524–10596)

**Key Functions**:

**`updateAtsBadge(score)` (lines 10533–10562)**:
```javascript
function updateAtsBadge(score) {
  const badge = document.getElementById('ats-score-badge');
  const valueEl = document.getElementById('ats-score-value');
  if (!badge || !valueEl) return;

  if (!score || typeof score.overall !== 'number') {
    badge.style.display = 'none';
    return;
  }

  const overall = Math.round(score.overall);
  valueEl.textContent = `${overall}%`;
  badge.style.display = 'flex';

  // Apply threshold colour class
  badge.classList.remove('score-high', 'score-medium', 'score-low');
  if (overall >= 75) {
    badge.classList.add('score-high');
  } else if (overall >= 50) {
    badge.classList.add('score-medium');
  } else {
    badge.classList.add('score-low');
  }

  // Update aria-label for accessibility
  badge.setAttribute(
    'aria-label',
    `ATS match score: ${overall}% (${score.basis || 'review'})`
  );
}
```

**`refreshAtsScore(basis)` (lines 10570–10588)**:
```javascript
async function refreshAtsScore(basis = 'review_checkpoint') {
  const sessionId = stateManager.getSessionId();
  if (!sessionId) return;
  try {
    const res = await fetch('/api/cv/ats-score', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({ session_id: sessionId, basis }),
    });
    if (!res.ok) return;
    const data = await res.json();
    if (data.ok && data.ats_score) {
      stateManager.setAtsScore(data.ats_score);
      updateAtsBadge(data.ats_score);
    }
  } catch (_e) {
    // Non-fatal — badge stays hidden or shows stale value
  }
}
```

**`scheduleAtsRefresh(basis)` (lines 10593–10596)**:
- Debounced version: waits 600ms after last call to batch updates during rapid review-decision sequences

**State Management** (in `/web/state-manager.js`, lines 70–120):
- `getAtsScore()` / `setAtsScore(score)` / `clearAtsScore()` manage the cached score
- Score persists in `localStorage` across page reloads

### 5. Existing Modal Dialog Patterns

**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/.claude/worktrees/gh-issues/web/index.html`

The codebase has 4 modal dialog patterns (all using the same structure):

**Pattern Structure**:
```html
<div class="modal-overlay" id="[name]-modal-overlay" style="display: none;" 
     role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="[id]-title" 
     onclick="if(event.target===this)close[Name]Modal()">
  <div class="modal" style="max-width: XXXpx; width: XX%;">
    <div class="modal-header" style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center;">
      <h2 id="[id]-title" style="margin:0;">Title</h2>
      <button onclick="close[Name]Modal()" style="background:none;border:none;font-size:1.4em;cursor:pointer;color:#64748b;" title="Close">&times;</button>
    </div>
    <div class="modal-body">
      <!-- Content -->
    </div>
    <div class="modal-footer" style="display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:center;">
      <!-- Buttons -->
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

**Example 1: Sessions Modal** (lines 197–216):
- Overlay ID: `sessions-modal-overlay`
- Body ID: `sessions-modal-body` (scrollable, max-height: 520px)
- Close function: `closeSessionsModal()`

**Example 2: Alert Modal** (lines 222–234):
- Overlay ID: `alert-modal-overlay`
- Message display: `#alert-modal-message`
- Single OK button closes it

**Example 3: Model Selection Modal** (lines 253–299):
- Overlay ID: `model-modal-overlay`
- Complex body with tabs/table
- Footer with action buttons + utility buttons (Test connection, Refresh prices)

**Example 4: Ownership Conflict Dialog** (lines 236–250):
- Overlay ID: `ownership-conflict-overlay`
- Three action buttons: Load Different, New Session, Take Over (primary)

**Key Pattern Features**:
- Backdrop click closes modal: `onclick="if(event.target===this)close...()"`
- Close button (×) in top-right of header
- ARIA attributes: `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, `aria-labelledby`
- Consistent CSS classes: `modal-overlay`, `modal`, `modal-header`, `modal-body`, `modal-footer`

```javascript
/**
 * ats-modals.js
 * ATS Report and Job Analysis modal dialogs + ATS Score tab renderer.
 *
 * DEPENDENCIES:
 * - app.js (for stateManager, tabData, htmlEscape / escapeHtml, populateAnalysisTab)
 * - index.html modal overlays: #ats-report-modal-overlay, #job-analysis-modal-overlay
 */

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// ATS Report Modal
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

/**
 * Open the ATS Report modal. Renders the cached ATS score from state, or
 * fetches a fresh score if none is cached.
 */
async function openAtsReportModal() {
  document.getElementById('ats-report-modal-overlay').style.display = 'flex';
  const body = document.getElementById('ats-report-modal-body');

  const cached = stateManager?.getAtsScore?.();
  if (cached) {
    body.innerHTML = _renderAtsReport(cached);
    return;
  }

  body.innerHTML = '<p style="padding:24px;text-align:center;color:#6b7280;">Fetching ATS score…</p>';
  try {
    const sessionId = stateManager?.getSessionId?.();
    const res = await fetch('/api/cv/ats-score', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({ session_id: sessionId, basis: 'review_checkpoint' }),
    });
    const data = await res.json();
    if (data.ok && data.ats_score) {
      stateManager?.setAtsScore?.(data.ats_score);
      if (typeof updateAtsBadge === 'function') updateAtsBadge(data.ats_score);
      body.innerHTML = _renderAtsReport(data.ats_score);
    } else {
      body.innerHTML = `<p style="padding:24px;color:#ef4444;">Could not load ATS report: ${escapeHtml(data.error || 'unknown error')}</p>`;
    }
  } catch (e) {
    body.innerHTML = `<p style="padding:24px;color:#ef4444;">Error: ${escapeHtml(e.message)}</p>`;
  }
}

function closeAtsReportModal() {
  document.getElementById('ats-report-modal-overlay').style.display = 'none';
}

/**
 * Render an ATS score object into HTML for the modal body.
 */
function _renderAtsReport(score) {
  const overall = Math.round(score.overall ?? 0);
  const hard = Math.round(score.hard_requirement_score ?? 0);
  const soft = Math.round(score.soft_requirement_score ?? 0);

  const scoreColor = overall >= 75 ? '#10b981' : overall >= 50 ? '#f59e0b' : '#ef4444';

  const keywords = score.keyword_status || [];
  const matchedKw = keywords.filter(k => k.found);
  const missingKw = keywords.filter(k => !k.found);

  const kwHtml = keywords.length === 0 ? '' : `
    <div style="margin-top:16px;">
      <h4 style="margin:0 0 8px;font-size:1rem;color:#334155;">Keywords</h4>
      <div style="display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:6px;">
        ${keywords.map(k => `
          <span style="padding:2px 8px;border-radius:12px;font-size:0.85em;
            background:${k.found ? '#dcfce7' : '#fee2e2'};
            color:${k.found ? '#166534' : '#991b1b'};">
            ${escapeHtml(k.keyword || k.term || '')}${k.rank ? ` #${k.rank}` : ''}
          </span>`).join('')}
      </div>
    </div>`;

  const sectionScores = score.section_scores || {};
  const sectHtml = Object.keys(sectionScores).length === 0 ? '' : `
    <div style="margin-top:16px;">
      <h4 style="margin:0 0 8px;font-size:1rem;color:#334155;">Section Scores</h4>
      <table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:0.9em;">
        ${Object.entries(sectionScores).map(([sec, val]) => `
          <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid #f1f5f9;">
            <td style="padding:4px 8px;text-transform:capitalize;color:#475569;">${escapeHtml(sec)}</td>
            <td style="padding:4px 8px;font-weight:600;color:#1e293b;">${Math.round(val)}%</td>
          </tr>`).join('')}
      </table>
    </div>`;

  return `
    <div style="padding:20px;">
      <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:24px;margin-bottom:20px;">
        <div style="font-size:2.4em;font-weight:700;color:${scoreColor};">${overall}%</div>
        <div>
          <div style="font-size:0.9em;color:#475569;">Hard requirements: <strong>${hard}%</strong></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.9em;color:#475569;">Preferred skills: <strong>${soft}%</strong></div>
          <div style="font-size:0.75em;color:#94a3b8;margin-top:2px;">Basis: ${escapeHtml(score.basis || 'review')}</div>
        </div>
      </div>
      ${kwHtml}
      ${sectHtml}
      ${missingKw.length > 0 ? `
        <div style="margin-top:16px;padding:12px;background:#fff7ed;border:1px solid #fed7aa;border-radius:6px;">
          <strong style="color:#9a3412;">Missing keywords (${missingKw.length}):</strong>
          <span style="color:#7c2d12;font-size:0.9em;"> ${missingKw.map(k => escapeHtml(k.keyword || k.term || '')).join(', ')}</span>
        </div>` : ''}
    </div>`;
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Job Analysis Modal
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

/**
 * Open the Job Analysis modal. Reuses tabData.analysis if available.
 */
function openJobAnalysisModal() {
  document.getElementById('job-analysis-modal-overlay').style.display = 'flex';
  const body = document.getElementById('job-analysis-modal-body');

  const analysis = window.tabData?.analysis;
  if (analysis) {
    // Render into a temp div using populateAnalysisTab's output pattern
    const tmp = document.createElement('div');
    tmp.style.padding = '20px';
    body.innerHTML = '';
    body.appendChild(tmp);
    // populateAnalysisTab writes to #document-content; use a shim
    _renderAnalysisIntoEl(tmp, analysis);
  } else {
    body.innerHTML = '<p style="padding:24px;text-align:center;color:#6b7280;">No job analysis available. Run job analysis first.</p>';
  }
}

function closeJobAnalysisModal() {
  document.getElementById('job-analysis-modal-overlay').style.display = 'none';
}

/**
 * Render job analysis data into an arbitrary container element.
 * Mirrors the structure from populateAnalysisTab in app.js.
 */
function _renderAnalysisIntoEl(el, result) {
  if (!result) {
    el.innerHTML = '<p style="color:#6b7280;">No analysis data.</p>';
    return;
  }

  const req = result.required_skills || [];
  const pref = result.preferred_skills || result.nice_to_have || [];
  const keywords = result.ats_keywords || [];
  const culture = result.culture_indicators || [];
  const mustHave = result.must_have_requirements || [];
  const missing = result.missing_required || [];

  el.innerHTML = `
    <div style="font-size:0.95em;line-height:1.6;">
      <div style="background:#f8fafc;border:1px solid #e2e8f0;border-radius:8px;padding:14px 16px;margin-bottom:14px;">
        <div style="font-size:1.1em;font-weight:700;color:#1e293b;">${escapeHtml(result.job_title || result.title || 'Role')}</div>
        ${result.company ? `<div style="color:#475569;">${escapeHtml(result.company)}</div>` : ''}
        ${result.domain ? `<span style="display:inline-block;margin-top:6px;padding:2px 8px;background:#dbeafe;color:#1e40af;border-radius:10px;font-size:0.8em;">${escapeHtml(result.domain)}</span>` : ''}
      </div>

      ${missing.length > 0 ? `
        <div style="background:#fff7ed;border:1px solid #fed7aa;border-radius:6px;padding:10px 14px;margin-bottom:14px;">
          <strong style="color:#9a3412;">⚠ Missing required skills:</strong>
          <div style="color:#7c2d12;margin-top:4px;">${missing.map(s => escapeHtml(s)).join(' · ')}</div>
        </div>` : ''}

      ${req.length > 0 ? `
        <div style="margin-bottom:14px;">
          <h4 style="margin:0 0 8px;font-size:0.95em;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.5px;color:#334155;">Required Skills</h4>
          <div style="display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:6px;">
            ${req.map(s => `<span style="padding:2px 10px;border-radius:12px;background:${missing.includes(s)?'#fee2e2':'#dcfce7'};color:${missing.includes(s)?'#991b1b':'#166534'};font-size:0.85em;">${escapeHtml(s)}</span>`).join('')}
          </div>
        </div>` : ''}

      ${pref.length > 0 ? `
        <div style="margin-bottom:14px;">
          <h4 style="margin:0 0 8px;font-size:0.95em;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.5px;color:#334155;">Preferred / Nice-to-have</h4>
          <ul style="margin:0;padding-left:18px;color:#475569;">${pref.map(s => `<li>${escapeHtml(s)}</li>`).join('')}</ul>
        </div>` : ''}

      ${keywords.length > 0 ? `
        <div style="margin-bottom:14px;">
          <h4 style="margin:0 0 8px;font-size:0.95em;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.5px;color:#334155;">ATS Keywords</h4>
          <div style="display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:6px;">
            ${keywords.map((k, i) => `<span style="padding:2px 8px;border-radius:12px;background:#f0f9ff;border:1px solid #bae6fd;color:#0369a1;font-size:0.82em;">${i < 5 ? `<strong>#${i+1}</strong> ` : ''}${escapeHtml(typeof k === 'string' ? k : k.keyword || k.term || '')}</span>`).join('')}
          </div>
        </div>` : ''}

      ${mustHave.length > 0 ? `
        <div style="margin-bottom:14px;">
          <h4 style="margin:0 0 8px;font-size:0.95em;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.5px;color:#334155;">Must-have Requirements</h4>
          <ul style="margin:0;padding-left:18px;color:#475569;">${mustHave.map(s => `<li>${escapeHtml(s)}</li>`).join('')}</ul>
        </div>` : ''}

      ${culture.length > 0 ? `
        <div>
          <h4 style="margin:0 0 8px;font-size:0.95em;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.5px;color:#334155;">Culture Indicators</h4>
          <ul style="margin:0;padding-left:18px;color:#475569;">${culture.map(s => `<li>${escapeHtml(s)}</li>`).join('')}</ul>
        </div>` : ''}
    </div>`;
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// ATS Score Tab (in customize phase)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

/**
 * Populate the ATS Score tab content area.
 * Called by app.js switchTab when case 'ats-score' is selected.
 */
async function populateAtsScoreTab() {
  const content = document.getElementById('document-content');
  if (!content) return;

  const cached = stateManager?.getAtsScore?.();
  if (cached) {
    content.innerHTML = `<div style="max-width:680px;margin:0 auto;">${_renderAtsReport(cached)}</div>`;
    return;
  }

  content.innerHTML = '<div class="empty-state"><div class="icon">📊</div><h3>ATS Score</h3><p>Complete job analysis and skills review to see your ATS score</p><button class="btn btn-primary" onclick="refreshAtsScore(\'review_checkpoint\').then(()=>populateAtsScoreTab())">Compute ATS Score</button></div>';
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Show/hide the modal shortcut buttons when ATS badge becomes visible
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

/**
 * Expose a hook so updateAtsBadge can reveal the shortcut buttons.
 * Called after updateAtsBadge shows the badge.
 */
function _syncAtsModalButtons(visible) {
  const reportBtn = document.getElementById('ats-report-btn');
  const analysisBtn = document.getElementById('job-analysis-btn');
  if (reportBtn) reportBtn.style.display = visible ? 'inline-block' : 'none';
  if (analysisBtn) analysisBtn.style.display = visible ? 'inline-block' : 'none';
}

// Patch updateAtsBadge (defined in app.js) to also toggle shortcut buttons.
// We wait for DOMContentLoaded so app.js has already defined the function.
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
  if (typeof updateAtsBadge !== 'function') return;
  const _orig = updateAtsBadge;
  window.updateAtsBadge = function(score) {
    _orig(score);
    const badge = document.getElementById('ats-score-badge');
    _syncAtsModalButtons(badge && badge.style.display !== 'none');
  };
});

```

```text
/**
 * Fetch ATS score from backend and update badge + state.
 * Safe to call at any phase — does nothing when no session/analysis available.
 *
 * @param {string} [basis]  "analysis" | "review_checkpoint" | "post_generation"
 */
async function refreshAtsScore(basis = 'review_checkpoint') {
  const sessionId = stateManager.getSessionId();
  if (!sessionId) return;
  try {
    const res = await fetch('/api/cv/ats-score', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({ session_id: sessionId, basis }),
    });
    if (!res.ok) return;
    const data = await res.json();
    if (data.ok && data.ats_score) {
      stateManager.setAtsScore(data.ats_score);
      updateAtsBadge(data.ats_score);
    }
  } catch (_e) {
    // Non-fatal — badge stays hidden or shows stale value
  }
}

// Debounced version: defers refresh 600ms after the last call.
// Used during rapid review-decision sequences to avoid per-click requests.
let _atsRefreshTimer = null;
function scheduleAtsRefresh(basis = 'review_checkpoint') {
  clearTimeout(_atsRefreshTimer);
  _atsRefreshTimer = setTimeout(() => refreshAtsScore(basis), 600);
}

// CJS export shim — no-op in browsers (module is undefined)
if (typeof module !== 'undefined') {
  module.exports = {
    showBulletReorder,
    formatSessionPhaseLabel,
    buildSessionSwitcherLabel,
    getActiveSessionOwnershipMeta,
    extractStructuredQuestionsFromAssistantText,
    updateAtsBadge,
    refreshAtsScore,
    scheduleAtsRefresh,
    saveSuggestedAchievementField,
    moveSuggestedAchievementRow,
    deleteSuggestedAchievement,
  };
}
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-21 02:53:55Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I can see the issue! At line 364:```css
page-break-after: always; /* Force a break after each defined page block */
```

This is applied to ALL `.page` elements within `@media print`. This means after `#page-two` (or `#page-three` if it's present), there's a `page-break-after: always` which forces a blank trailing page.

The fix: 
1. Remove `page-break-after: always` from the last page element in the document
2. Since we don't know which is the last page at CSS time, we can use `page-break-after: always` on `#page-one` and `#page-two`, but not on the last page. We can do this with CSS using `:last-child` or by not applying the rule to the last `.page` element.


Let me examine the full CSS to see exactly how the page breaks are structured and identify which pages need the break applied.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Line 2684: `pdf_path = pdf_files[0] if pdf_files else None`

The issue is that `sorted(output_dir.glob('*.pdf'))` sorts alphabetically. The dated initial PDF would be something like `CV_CompanyName_Role_20260320.pdf` and the final PDF after gh-29 fix would be `CV_Acme_Engineer_2026-03-20.pdf`. Alphabetical sort might pick the wrong one.

The fix: prefer a file named `CV_final.pdf` or any PDF that contains 'final' in the name. But after our gh-29 fix, final files are now named descriptively. 


So I should sort by modification time instead—picking the most recently modified PDF would reliably get the final output, since that's what was generated last. The challenge is that after the gh-29 changes, the filename is descriptive rather than explicitly marked as final, so I need to rely on file timestamps rather than naming conventions.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-21 02:55:21Z)**_

The fix: sort PDFs by modification time (most recent first) instead of alphabetically — the final PDF is always the newest.

`.job-header` already has `justify-content: space-between` which should push `.job-role` to the left and `.job-company` to the right. But the issue says company is not right-aligned.

Looking at the CSS: `.job-header` has `display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: baseline`. With `space-between`, the first item goes to the left, last to the right. So `.job-role` should be left and `.job-company` should be right.

But if there's no flex, or if the text wraps, this might not work as expected. The issue says "company name appears inline-left next to the role rather than right-aligned." This suggests that maybe in practice, the flex isn't working as expected — perhaps the job-header is inside a container that doesn't have full width, or the flex children have some behavior that makes them align left.
 
I think the real issue might be that `justify-content: space-between` isn't actually working in this context, or the company name needs explicit right alignment. I should add `text-align: right` to `.job-company` and possibly `margin-left: auto` to ensure it gets pushed to the right side, which would be a more reliable approach than relying solely on the flex container's justify-content property.
</details></think>
